## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# DEV A — 3D Engine, Stylized Model & Animation Orchestration
2	
3	**Branch** : `feat/3d`
4	**Files owned** : tout `src/components/Scene3D/*` (House.tsx, Panels.tsx, Inverter, Battery, HeatPump, Wallbox, Heatmap, Sun, CameraRig, Orchestrator) + `public/sounds/*`
5	**Pair sync** :
6	- Sat 17:00 with Dev D — handoff `analysis.json` (D fournit, A consomme)
7	- Sat 22:00 with Dev C — Orchestrator ↔ store ↔ animations
8	**Frozen** : `lib/types.ts` after Sat 15:30 (B+C pair)
9	**Charge estimée** : ~10h utiles
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Le rôle, en 1 phrase
14	
15	Tu **possèdes tout le visuel** : tu génères le modèle 3D stylé **runtime en r3f** depuis le `analysis.json` que Dev D te livre (footprint + faces toiture + obstructions), tu rends les composants énergétiques (panneaux, batterie, etc.), tu pilotes la caméra cinématique et l'orchestrator d'animation. **3D Tiles brut n'est jamais affiché.** Style cible : architectural mockup / Apple Keynote.
16	
17	---
18	
19	## ⚠️ Architecture clé : la mesh stylée est **générée par l'AI agent** depuis l'analyse de Dev D
20	
21	Dev D livre **uniquement** `public/baked/{house}-analysis.json` (footprint + faces + obstructions + panneaux + buildingFootprint). À partir de ces inputs + le résultat 3D Tiles, **l'AI génère le modèle stylé final** que le user voit. Dans la narrative démo, ça correspond à la phase **RENDER** de l'agent trace.
22	
23	Tu as **2 façons d'implémenter** ça (à toi de choisir samedi selon ton temps) :
24	
25	### Option A — Procédural pur (simple, safe, déjà câblé)
26	La fonction procédurale dans `House.tsx` lit `analysis.json` et construit la mesh r3f à la volée :
27	- Volumes de murs depuis `buildingFootprint.size`
28	- Plans de toit depuis `faces[].vertices` (BufferGeometry triangulée)
29	- Cheminées/lucarnes depuis `obstructions[]`
30	- Tout en `MeshToonMaterial` blanc cassé + outline shader noir au composer level
31	
32	→ Le squelette est déjà dans `House.tsx`. Pitch : *"L'AI agent génère le modèle depuis l'analyse géométrique (procedural code triggered by the agent)"*.
33	
34	### Option B — Gemini Vision + procédural (stretch goal, vraiment "AI-driven")
35	Pendant la phase RENDER, on appelle Gemini Vision avec un screenshot de la mesh photogrammétrique + analysis.json. Gemini retourne des params (couleurs murs, type de toit, balcons, etc.) → la fonction procédurale les utilise.
36	
37	→ +2h dev en pair avec Dev B (qui possède `lib/gemini.ts`). Pitch beaucoup plus fort : *"Gemini Vision interprète la photogrammétrie et génère le mockup stylé"*.
38	
39	### Reco
40	Commence Option A samedi (déjà prêt). Si tu finis tes autres tâches dimanche matin avant 8h et qu'il te reste de l'énergie, upgrade vers Option B. **Pas obligatoire** : la phase RENDER reste impressionnante en procédural.
41	
42	Avantage commun : pas de GLB à baker, pas de fichier binaire, le modèle s'adapte automatiquement à n'importe quel `analysis.json`.
43	
44	---
45	
46	## Setup (5 min)
47	
48	```bash
49	git checkout feat/3d
50	pnpm install
51	pnpm dev
52	```
53	
54	`/design/brandenburg` doit afficher le **placeholder procédural** (cube blanc + toit rouge incliné) qui est dans `House.tsx`. Tu peux dev tout ton code dessus dès maintenant ; quand Dev D livre `brandenburg-stylized.glb`, le composant le détecte automatiquement et bascule.
55	
56	À lire :
57	- https://github.com/pmndrs/postprocessing (Bloom, ToneMapping, OutlineEffect)
58	- https://drei.docs.pmnd.rs/ (`<Outlines>`, `<Environment>`, `<MeshTransmissionMaterial>`)
59	
60	---
61	
62	## Tâches (chronologiques avec ETA)
63	
64	| # | Tâche | ETA | Critique ? |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| A1 | **Style "architectural mockup"** : `MeshToonMaterial` blanc cassé sur la maison + outline shader noir (drei `<Outlines>` ou `OutlineEffect` postprocessing). Sol = plan blanc avec contact shadow. Background gradient ciel doux. | 2h | wow |
67	| A2 | **Photorealism subtle** : `<Environment preset="apartment">` (lumière douce diffuse, pas trop chaude car notre style est minimaliste, PAS sunset comme avant) + Bloom léger + `ACESFilmic` tonemapping | 45min | wow |
68	| A3 | **CameraRig.tsx** : finir GSAP timeline (aerial dive 80→30→25 vers le centre du `buildingFootprint`, lookAt centré sur le toit) | 1h | wow |
69	| A4 | **Sun.tsx** : SunCalc → directionalLight position animée 12s pendant `phase=agent-running` (la lumière bouge subtilement sur le modèle stylé) | 45min | wow |
70	| A5 | **Composants 3D** (Inverter/Battery/HeatPump/Wallbox/Panels) en cohérence stylée — toon shaders, pas PBR. Animations spécifiques par step Orchestrator | 2h | démo |
71	| A6 | **Heatmap.tsx** : injecter vertex colors depuis `{house}-analysis.json` (faces[].yieldKwhPerSqm) sur le toit du modèle stylé. Turbo gradient subtle (pas trop saturé pour respecter le style architectural) | 1h | wow |
72	| A7 | **Sounds Howler.js** : sourcer 6 mp3 (whoosh/scan/tick/paint/place/chime) gratuits sur freesound.org, brancher sur les steps Orchestrator | 45min | wow |
73	| A8 | **FPS bench** sur MacBook moyen Sun 01:00. Si <60fps : alléger outline shader ou bloom | 30min | démo |
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Style guide (important)
78	
79	Le style doit rester cohérent. Évite les pièges :
80	
81	| À faire | À éviter |
82	|---|---|
83	| 🟢 Volume blanc cassé (`#f5f1ea` ou `#fafafa`) | 🔴 Couleurs saturées photoréalistes |
84	| 🟢 Outline shader noir (épaisseur 2-3px) | 🔴 Textures bumpy / photogrammétrie |
85	| 🟢 Sol clair avec subtle contact shadow | 🔴 Background HDR sunset bumpy |
86	| 🟢 Panneaux bleu profond `#1a3a6e` métallique | 🔴 Panneaux PBR ultra-réalistes (clash) |
87	| 🟢 Animations easing cubic, GSAP | 🔴 Bounce trop cartoony |
88	| 🟢 Heatmap turbo subtle (alpha bas) | 🔴 Heatmap saturé qui mange le toit |
89	
90	→ Style cible : **Norman Foster rendering / Apple Maps low-poly**.
91	
92	---
93	
94	## Dépendances bloquantes
95	
96	- **Bloque** : Dev C (Orchestrator drives ses composants UI)
97	- **Bloqué par** :
98	  - Dev D pour `public/baked/{house}-stylized.glb` (placeholder procédural en place → tu peux bosser parallèle dès Sat 15:00)
99	  - Dev D pour `public/baked/{house}-analysis.json` (mock Brandenburg déjà committé pour les modulePositions + faces)
100	
101	---
102	
103	## Fichiers à modifier
104	
105	```
106	src/components/Scene3D/Scene3D.tsx        — assemble (déjà câblé, ajouter outline composer)
107	src/components/Scene3D/House.tsx          — déjà câblé pour stylized.glb + procedural fallback
108	src/components/Scene3D/Sun.tsx            — SunCalc useFrame
109	src/components/Scene3D/CameraRig.tsx      — GSAP timeline (squelette OK)
110	src/components/Scene3D/Inverter.tsx       — pop-in animation, toon material
111	src/components/Scene3D/Battery.tsx        — slide-up animation, toon
112	src/components/Scene3D/HeatPump.tsx       — fade-in + scale, toon
113	src/components/Scene3D/Wallbox.tsx        — fade-in, toon
114	src/components/Scene3D/Panels.tsx         — drop + bounce per panel, panneaux toon-bleus
115	src/components/Scene3D/Heatmap.tsx        — vertex colors apply (lit analysis.json)
116	src/components/Scene3D/Orchestrator.tsx   — step callbacks → animations
117	public/sounds/*.mp3                       — sourcer 6 fichiers
118	```
119	
120	---
121	
122	## Critères d'acceptation
123	
124	- [ ] La séquence agent (~22s) joue de bout en bout sans crash sur `/design/brandenburg`
125	- [ ] 60 fps stable sur MacBook moyen pendant la démo
126	- [ ] Style architectural mockup cohérent (volume blanc + outline + sol clair)
127	- [ ] Click sur un toggle (EV/HP/Battery/Wallbox) ajoute/retire le bon objet 3D en live
128	- [ ] Sons subtle synced avec chaque step
129	- [ ] Quand Dev D livre `brandenburg-stylized.glb`, ça remplace le placeholder procédural sans toucher au code
130	
131	---
132	
133	## Plan B si bloqué
134	
135	| Cas | Plan B |
136	|---|---|
137	| Outline shader trop lourd | drei `<Outlines>` simple sur les meshes (moins joli mais marche partout) |
138	| Stylized.glb pas livré par D | Garder le procedural placeholder (déjà en place dans House.tsx). Démo continue, look légèrement moins customisé par maison. |
139	| InstancedMesh + animation trop lourd | Passer en `<group>` de panneaux normaux (acceptable jusqu'à ~30 panneaux) |
140	| Heatmap vertex colors plante | Skip carrément — le brief ne le rend pas obligatoire |
141	| Sons impossibles à sourcer en 45min | Skip Howler, animation visuelle suffit |
142	
143	---
144	
145	## Hand-off
146	
147	- Dev C consomme tes animations via le store (`phase` + `agentSteps`)
148	- Dev D te fournit `public/baked/{house}-stylized.glb` + `analysis.json`
149	
150	Si tu finis tôt : aide Dev C sur le polish UI ou Dev D sur le `generate-stylized.ts`.
151

Dev D livre **uniquement** `public/baked/{house}-analysis.json` (footprint + faces + obstructions + panneaux + buildingFootprint). À partir de ces inputs + le résultat 3D Tiles, **l'AI génère le modèle stylé final** que le user voit. Dans la narrative démo, ça correspond à la phase **RENDER** de l'agent trace.

Tu as **2 façons d'implémenter** ça (à toi de choisir samedi selon ton temps) :

### Option A — Procédural pur (simple, safe, déjà câblé)
La fonction procédurale dans `House.tsx` lit `analysis.json` et construit la mesh r3f à la volée :
- Volumes de murs depuis `buildingFootprint.size`
- Plans de toit depuis `faces[].vertices` (BufferGeometry triangulée)
- Cheminées/lucarnes depuis `obstructions[]`
- Tout en `MeshToonMaterial` blanc cassé + outline shader noir au composer level

→ Le squelette est déjà dans `House.tsx`. Pitch : *"L'AI agent génère le modèle depuis l'analyse géométrique (procedural code triggered by the agent)"*.

### Option B — Gemini Vision + procédural (stretch goal, vraiment "AI-driven")

- https://github.com/pmndrs/postprocessing (Bloom, ToneMapping, OutlineEffect)

| # | Tâche | ETA | Critique ? |

| A2 | **Photorealism subtle** : `<Environment preset="apartment">` (lumière douce diffuse, pas trop chaude car notre style est minimaliste, PAS sunset comme avant) + Bloom léger + `ACESFilmic` tonemapping | 45min | wow |
| A3 | **CameraRig.tsx** : finir GSAP timeline (aerial dive 80→30→25 vers le centre du `buildingFootprint`, lookAt centré sur le toit) | 1h | wow |
| A4 | **Sun.tsx** : SunCalc → directionalLight position animée 12s pendant `phase=agent-running` (la lumière bouge subtilement sur le modèle stylé) | 45min | wow |
| A5 | **Composants 3D** (Inverter/Battery/HeatPump/Wallbox/Panels) en cohérence stylée — toon shaders, pas PBR. Animations spécifiques par step Orchestrator | 2h | démo |
| A6 | **Heatmap.tsx** : injecter vertex colors depuis `{house}-analysis.json` (faces[].yieldKwhPerSqm) sur le toit du modèle stylé. Turbo gradient subtle (pas trop saturé pour respecter le style architectural) | 1h | wow |
| A7 | **Sounds Howler.js** : sourcer 6 mp3 (whoosh/scan/tick/paint/place/chime) gratuits sur freesound.org, brancher sur les steps Orchestrator | 45min | wow |
| A8 | **FPS bench** sur MacBook moyen Sun 01:00. Si <60fps : alléger outline shader ou bloom | 30min | démo |

## Style guide (important)

Le style doit rester cohérent. Évite les pièges :

| À faire | À éviter |
|---|---|
| 🟢 Volume blanc cassé (`#f5f1ea` ou `#fafafa`) | 🔴 Couleurs saturées photoréalistes |
| 🟢 Outline shader noir (épaisseur 2-3px) | 🔴 Textures bumpy / photogrammétrie |
| 🟢 Sol clair avec subtle contact shadow | 🔴 Background HDR sunset bumpy |
| 🟢 Panneaux bleu profond `#1a3a6e` métallique | 🔴 Panneaux PBR ultra-réalistes (clash) |
| 🟢 Animations easing cubic, GSAP | 🔴 Bounce trop cartoony |
| 🟢 Heatmap turbo subtle (alpha bas) | 🔴 Heatmap saturé qui mange le toit |

→ Style cible : **Norman Foster rendering / Apple Maps low-poly**.

- **Bloque** : Dev C (Orchestrator drives ses composants UI)

## 2. `Scene3D.tsx` — apartment env + bloom + ACES + contact shadow
Replace the existing `Environment preset="sunset"` and bare `OrbitControls` with:
- `<Environment preset="apartment" background={false} />` (soft diffuse light, NOT sunset).
- A subtle gradient sky: a large inverted sphere or `<color attach="background" args={['#e8eef5']} />` on the scene — pick whichever is simpler and renders correctly (color attach is fine).
- `<ContactShadows position={[0, 0, 0]} opacity={0.4} scale={40} blur={2.5} far={20} />` from drei for the soft ground shadow.
- A faint ground plane: `<mesh rotation={[-Math.PI/2, 0, 0]} receiveShadow><planeGeometry args={[200, 200]} /><meshStandardMaterial color="#fafafa" /></mesh>`.
- `<EffectComposer>` from `@react-three/postprocessing` wrapping `<Bloom luminanceThreshold={0.9} intensity={0.4} mipmapBlur />` and `<ToneMapping mode={ToneMappingMode.ACES_FILMIC} />`. Import `ToneMappingMode` from `postprocessing`.
- Keep `<Sun />` and `<House houseId={houseId} />` rendering as before.
- Keep `<OrbitControls makeDefault enableDamping />` for now — the lead will swap it for `<CameraRig />` later. Leave a comment line `// TODO replaced by CameraRig in final integration`.
- Keep the existing `Suspense` boundary, `shadows` on Canvas, camera position `[40, 30, 40]` fov `35`, and `gl={{ antialias: true, toneMappingExposure: 1.1 }}`.

# Style guide (from DEV_A.md, must respect)
- 🟢 Off-white volume `#f5f1ea`, black outlines (~2px), light ground, apartment HDR.
- 🔴 NO saturated colors, NO sunset preset, NO bumpy textures.

# When done
1. Make sure `pnpm tsc --noEmit` passes for the two files you touched (run it; report any error you can't fix).
2. Send me (`team-lead`) a short message via SendMessage summarizing what you changed and any deviation from the brief. Then mark your task done.

You do not need to start the dev server — the team-lead will integrate and verify. Don't touch git (no commits, no branch ops).

# What to deliver

## 1. `CameraRig.tsx` — GSAP cinematic dive
A new `'use client'` component that:
- Imports `useThree` from `@react-three/fiber` and `gsap` from `gsap`.
- Imports `useStore` from `@/lib/store` to read `phase`.
- Default-exports a named `CameraRig` component (no props).
- On mount, captures `camera` from `useThree`.
- Uses `useEffect` keyed on `phase`. When phase becomes `'agent-running'`:
  - Kill any existing tween (`gsap.killTweensOf(camera.position)` and `gsap.killTweensOf(camera)`).
  - Start a single GSAP timeline that animates `camera.position` from `[80, 60, 80]` → `[30, 25, 30]` → `[25, 20, 25]` over ~10s with `power2.inOut` easing, pausing briefly between segments.
  - During the tween, in an `onUpdate`, call `camera.lookAt(0, 3, 0)` and `camera.updateProjectionMatrix()` to keep the lookAt centered on the roof of the building footprint (origin, slight Y offset).
- Returns `null` (no JSX). It only mutates the camera.
- Cleanup: on unmount or phase change, kill the tween.

## 2. `Sun.tsx` — SunCalc-driven directional light
Rewrite the placeholder to:
- Use `useFrame` from `@react-three/fiber` and `SunCalc` from `suncalc`.
- Read `phase` from `useStore`.
- Hold a `directionalLight` ref (`useRef<DirectionalLight>(null)`).
- During `phase === 'agent-running'`, advance a virtual time-of-day across ~12s (e.g., from 09:00 to 16:00 local time at lat 52.52, lng 13.40 — Berlin, since the demo house is Brandenburg). Compute `SunCalc.getPosition(virtualDate, lat, lng)` each frame; convert azimut+altitude to a direction vector and place the light at `direction * 30` so shadows track the sun.
- Keep `intensity={3}`, `castShadow`, `shadow-mapSize={[2048, 2048]}`, plus `shadow-bias={-0.0005}`.
- When `phase !== 'agent-running'`, freeze at a default mid-morning position (don't keep updating).
- Track elapsed time with a `useRef<number>(0)` accumulator updated via `useFrame((_, delta) => ...)`.

Math hint for converting SunCalc output (azimut from south, clockwise; altitude from horizon):
```
const x = Math.sin(azimuth) * Math.cos(altitude);
const y = Math.sin(altitude);
const z = Math.cos(azimuth) * Math.cos(altitude);
light.position.set(x*30, y*30 + 5, z*30);
```
(Adjust sign of azimuth if shadows go the wrong way — SunCalc returns south=0, west=π/2.)

'use client';

import { useGLTF, Outlines } from '@react-three/drei';
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import { Mesh, MeshToonMaterial } from 'three';
import type { HouseId } from '@/lib/types';

const PATHS: Record<HouseId, string> = {
  brandenburg: '/models/brandenburg.glb',
  hamburg: '/models/hamburg.glb',
  'north-germany': '/models/north-germany.glb',
  ruhr: '/models/ruhr.glb',
};

interface Props {
  houseId: HouseId;
}

export function House({ houseId }: Props) {
  const { scene } = useGLTF(PATHS[houseId]);

  const stylized = useMemo(() => {
    const cloned = scene.clone(true);
    cloned.traverse((obj) => {
      if ((obj as Mesh).isMesh) {
        const mesh = obj as Mesh;
        mesh.material = new MeshToonMaterial({ color: '#f5f1ea' });
        mesh.castShadow = true;
        mesh.receiveShadow = true;
      }
    });
    return cloned;
  }, [scene]);

  return (
    <primitive object={stylized}>
      <Outlines thickness={2} color="black" />
    </primitive>
  );
}

// Preload all houses for smoother demo transitions
Object.values(PATHS).forEach((p) => useGLTF.preload(p));

{"type":"permission_request","request_id":"perm-1777144156069-lx2cllo","agent_id":"stylist","tool_name":"Write","tool_use_id":"REDACTED","description":"Write a file to the local filesystem.","input":{"file_path":"/Users/lionelleguier/reonic-hackathon/reonic-hackathon/src/components/Scene3D/House.tsx","content":"// House GLB loader — OWNED by Dev A
'use client';

import { useGLTF, Outlines } from '@react-three/drei';
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import { Mesh, MeshToonMaterial } from 'three';
import type { HouseId } from '@/lib/types';

const PATHS: Record<HouseId, string> = {
  brandenburg: '/models/brandenburg.glb',
  hamburg: '/models/hamburg.glb',
  'north-germany': '/models/north-germany.glb',
  ruhr: '/models/ruhr.glb',
};

interface Props {
  houseId: HouseId;
}

export function House({ houseId }: Props) {
  const { scene } = useGLTF(PATHS[houseId]);

  const stylized = useMemo(() => {
    const cloned = scene.clone(true);
    cloned.traverse((obj) => {
      if ((obj as Mesh).isMesh) {
        const mesh = obj as Mesh;
        mesh.material = new MeshToonMaterial({ color: '#f5f1ea' });
        mesh.castShadow = true;
        mesh.receiveShadow = true;
      }
    });
    return cloned;
  }, [scene]);

  return (
    <primitive object={stylized}>
      <Outlines thickness={2} color="black" />
    </primitive>
  );
}

// Preload all houses for smoother demo transitions
Object.values(PATHS).forEach((p) => useGLTF.preload(p));
"},"permission_suggestions":[{"type":"setMode","mode":"acceptEdits","destination":"session"}]}

1	// Zustand state machine — OWNED by Dev C
2	// Read freely from anywhere. Writes only via the action methods below.
3	
4	import { create } from 'zustand';
5	import type {
6	  AppPhase,
7	  HouseId,
8	  CustomerProfile,
9	  DesignRefinements,
10	  DesignResult,
11	  AgentStep,
12	} from './types';
13	
14	interface AppState {
15	  // Phase
16	  phase: AppPhase;
17	  setPhase: (phase: AppPhase) => void;
18	
19	  // House selection
20	  selectedHouse: HouseId | null;
21	  selectHouse: (id: HouseId) => void;
22	
23	  // Customer profile (auto-filled, then editable)
24	  profile: CustomerProfile | null;
25	  setProfile: (p: CustomerProfile) => void;
26	  updateProfileField: <K extends keyof CustomerProfile>(
27	    key: K,
28	    value: CustomerProfile[K],
29	  ) => void;
30	
31	  // Refinements (post-design)
32	  refinements: DesignRefinements;
33	  setRefinement: <K extends keyof DesignRefinements>(
34	    key: K,
35	    value: DesignRefinements[K],
36	  ) => void;
37	
38	  // Design result (from /api/design)
39	  design: DesignResult | null;
40	  setDesign: (d: DesignResult | null) => void;
41	
42	  // Agent sequence
43	  agentSteps: AgentStep[];
44	  setAgentSteps: (s: AgentStep[]) => void;
45	  updateStepStatus: (id: string, status: AgentStep['status']) => void;
46	
47	  // Reset
48	  reset: () => void;
49	}
50	
51	const defaultRefinements: DesignRefinements = {
52	  includeBattery: true,
53	  includeHeatPump: true,
54	  includeWallbox: false,
55	};
56	
57	export const useStore = create<AppState>((set) => ({
58	  phase: 'idle',
59	  setPhase: (phase) => set({ phase }),
60	
61	  selectedHouse: null,
62	  selectHouse: (id) => set({ selectedHouse: id, phase: 'house-selected' }),
63	
64	  profile: null,
65	  setProfile: (profile) => set({ profile }),
66	  updateProfileField: (key, value) =>
67	    set((s) => ({ profile: s.profile ? { ...s.profile, [key]: value } : null })),
68	
69	  refinements: defaultRefinements,
70	  setRefinement: (key, value) =>
71	    set((s) => ({ refinements: { ...s.refinements, [key]: value } })),
72	
73	  design: null,
74	  setDesign: (design) => set({ design }),
75	
76	  agentSteps: [],
77	  setAgentSteps: (agentSteps) => set({ agentSteps }),
78	  updateStepStatus: (id, status) =>
79	    set((s) => ({
80	      agentSteps: s.agentSteps.map((step) =>
81	        step.id === id ? { ...step, status } : step,
82	      ),
83	    })),
84	
85	  reset: () =>
86	    set({
87	      phase: 'idle',
88	      selectedHouse: null,
89	      profile: null,
90	      refinements: defaultRefinements,
91	      design: null,
92	      agentSteps: [],
93	    }),
94	}));

Mais deux endpoints renvoient **502 (Bad Gateway)** — handlers compilent OK mais l'API en aval échoue :
- `POST /api/trellis 502` → call fal.ai échoue malgré la clé 69 chars (clé invalide/expirée, ou rate limit, ou input invalide)
- `GET /api/aerial 502` → call Google Maps 3D Tiles échoue (clé sans 3D Tiles activé / sans billing / referrer-restricted)

Tu peux tester l'UI sur http://localhost:3000 ; ces deux endpoints crieront. Si tu veux que je creuse les 502 (récupérer le vrai message d'erreur fal.ai et Google Maps), dis-le-moi et j'ajoute du logging dans les handlers — ou tu peux essayer toi-même un curl direct fal.ai pour valider la clé.
